mnemonics.title: Memory Types Remember
mnemonics.content: RAM IS VOLATILE: Random Access Memory Is Subject To Vanishing In Power OFF. ROM RETAINS: Read Only Memory Retains Everything Always And Needs No Power.

mnemonics.title: SRAM vs DRAM
mnemonics.content: S FOR SPEED: SRAM Is Super Fast. D FOR DRAIN: DRAM Drains Capacitors And Needs Refresh.

mnemonics.title: Memory Hierarchy Order
mnemonics.content: RIGS CAN HANDLE FULL RAW DATA: Registers → Internal cache → Main memory → Secondary storage → Magnetic tape → Optical storage → WAN.

mnemonics.title: ROM Types
mnemonics.content: PROM IS SIMPLE: Programmable Once. EPROM NEEDS UV: Erasable By UV Light. EEPROM ELECTRIC: Electrically Erasable. FLASH FAST: Fast Block Erasure.

acronyms.usage: Primary volatile memory for working data storage
Non-volatile memory for firmware and boot programs
Fast cache memory using flip-flops, no refresh needed
Main memory using capacitors, needs periodic refresh
ROM that can be programmed once after manufacture
ROM erasable using ultraviolet light and reprogrammable

acronyms.expansion: Electrically Erasable Programmable Read Only Memory
acronyms.usage: ROM electrically erasable and reprogrammable in circuit
Executes instructions and controls memory operations
Allows devices to transfer data without CPU intervention
Cache for virtual to physical address translation

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A PYRAMID: Top (smallest, fastest): Registers. Middle: Cache. Bottom: RAM. Base: Hard Disk. Each level wider than above, like an inverted pyramid showing growth in size but decrease in speed.

visualTips.visualize: THINK OF MAIL SYSTEM: Address bus is like postal code selecting neighborhood (chip), then street address selects house (memory location). Decoder is like mail sorter reading address and routing to correct destination.
DRAW NUMBER 1234 ON PAPER: Big-Endian writes as 1-2-3-4 (left to right). Little-Endian writes as 4-3-2-1 (right to left). In memory, each digit is a byte address.
DIRECT: Each floor has one specific apartment number. SET-ASSOCIATIVE: Each floor has few apartments with same number. FULLY-ASSOCIATIVE: Any apartment can be anywhere, search all.

analogies.analogy: LIBRARY ORGANIZATION: Librarian's desk has frequently used books (registers/cache). Stack area has popular books (RAM). Storage room has all books (hard disk). You get fastest access at desk but limited space.
FLIP-FLOP VS CAPACITOR: SRAM is like a light switch that stays in position (flip-flop). DRAM is like a leaky bucket needing periodic water refill (refresh) to maintain state.
HOTEL ROOM ADDRESSING: Address is room number, each room is memory location. Byte addressing = each bed is addressable. Word addressing = entire room is addressable unit.

MULTI-LANE HIGHWAY: Single lane (non-interleaved) = cars wait for each other. Multi-lane (interleaved) = cars use different lanes simultaneously, traffic flows faster. Consecutive addresses use different banks.
